workLog.task: Issue #1987: agents invoke skill scripts by the wrong name (e.g. get_unresolved_threads.py for get_unresolved_review_threads.py); nothing static catches the orphan reference
workLog.outcome: Extended the existing orphan-ref-validator (an approved capability) with SKILL_SCRIPT_REF_RE, extract_skill_script_refs, and _check_skill_script_refs that detect references to .claude/skills/**/scripts/**.py (and the copilot mirror) in both backticked and bare python3-command forms, checking existence against the working tree. The existing SCRIPT_REF_RE required backticks and only the build/scripts|scripts/validation|scripts prefixes, so it missed this class. Running the new check across the repo surfaced two real orphans in cva-analysis SKILL.md (generate-cva-template.py, export-cva-matrix.py document usage for scripts that were never implemented); fixed the doc to mark them planned without invocable paths. Added tests in the CI-collected tests/ location plus the skill-local and copilot copies. Bumped plugin manifests: project-toolkit (.claude) 0.4.24 -> 0.4.26 and the Copilot mirror 0.4.35 -> 0.4.37.
workLog.evidence: pytest tests/skills/orphan-ref-validator -> 8 passed; skill-local + copilot -> 56 passed; scanner reports 0 skill-script orphans repo-wide after the cva-analysis fix

nextSteps: Open PR with Fixes #1987 (sub-failure 1: wrong script name)
Sub-failure 2 (JSON-shape: inconsistent Data envelope across github scripts) is a separate output-contract standardization, out of scope here
Optional follow-up: wire the /build gate to scan agents/commands/skill-descriptions by default so the guard fires on authored refs, not only on demand
